touchpoints.label: Tutorials and onboarding
touchpoints.description: Does this feature need new tutorial steps, glossary updates, or guided surfacing?

touchpoints.label: UI and player readability
touchpoints.description: Does the player-facing UI explain the feature, surface state clearly, and avoid hidden rules?

touchpoints.label: Content and balance
touchpoints.description: Does the feature connect to content generation, balance passes, encounter tables, and tuning hooks?

touchpoints.label: AI and systemic behavior
touchpoints.description: Does AI, encounter logic, or autoplay understand the feature well enough to use or react to it?

touchpoints.label: Meta progression and persistence
touchpoints.description: Does the feature need unlocks, save data, dailies, run history, or long-term progression hooks?

touchpoints.label: Telemetry and debugging
touchpoints.description: Does the feature emit enough state or logs to debug balancing and regressions later?

touchpoints.label: Automated tests
touchpoints.description: Does the feature have baseline unit or integration coverage for the risky logic?

touchpoints.label: Audio and feedback
touchpoints.description: Does the feature need SFX, music, or stronger feedback hooks to feel intentional?

touchpoints.label: Narrative and atmosphere
touchpoints.description: Does the feature connect to worldbuilding, NPC reactions, or atmospheric delivery?

touchpoints.label: Docs and workflow
touchpoints.description: Is the feature documented well enough for future contributors to extend it safely?

features.title: Build and runtime guardrails
features.summary: Protect the content pipeline and runtime from silent failures with CI, tests, and crash recovery.

ui.notes: The app now has a visible recovery path instead of a white screen.

content_balance.notes: Generated content is validated and no longer silently deploys stale data.

telemetry.notes: This pass focuses on prevention and recovery rather than production analytics.

tests.notes: Baseline content and progression tests were added.

docs.notes: README and workflow docs were updated to match the real build path.

features.title: Tutorial onboarding
features.summary: Guided tutorial runs for combat basics and advanced mechanics.

tutorials.notes: This system is the tutorial layer itself.

ui.notes: Tutorial state is surfaced through overlays and action gating.

content_balance.notes: Tutorial decks and encounters are curated for specific teaching moments.

ai.notes: No autonomous agent needs to consume tutorial state yet.

progression.notes: Completion is persisted in local storage and surfaced in the app.

telemetry.notes: We do not yet log tutorial completion or drop-off points for balancing.

tests.notes: Tutorial flow now has direct regression coverage, including the pressure-systems lesson and scripted reforge transition.

audio.notes: Tutorial-specific success/error cues are still missing.

narrative.notes: This is teaching-focused for now rather than story delivery.

docs.notes: The integration-pipeline docs now call out tutorial and autoplay retrofit hotspots so new mechanics get onboarded intentionally.

features.title: Meta progression and run modes
features.summary: Starter profiles, ascensions, challenges, daily runs, and persistent unlock tracking.

tutorials.notes: The player still needs onboarding for classes, dailies, and challenge tradeoffs.

ui.notes: The mode selection and unlock state are already surfaced in the menu flow.

content_balance.notes: Profiles and challenge modifiers feed run configuration directly.

ai.notes: Run profile composition flows through the existing run setup path.

progression.notes: This is the core persistence layer for unlocks and run history.

telemetry.notes: We still need a cleaner debugging/reporting view for why unlock thresholds are firing.

tests.notes: Meta progression and run profile coverage is in place.

audio.notes: No bespoke audio hooks are required for the underlying rules.

narrative.notes: Future NPC/fixer commentary should react to run-mode history and unlocks.

docs.notes: Contributor docs still need a concise explanation of how mode data composes.

features.title: Boss directives and contextual events
features.summary: Boss phase gimmicks, encounter directives, and contextual event selection to deepen runs.

tutorials.notes: We should add a guided explanation for directives and phase bosses.

ui.notes: Directive summaries and encounter hints are already surfaced in combat.

content_balance.notes: The systems connect to encounter tables, event picking, and combat resolution.

ai.notes: Encounter logic and enemy turns both understand the new directive hooks.

progression.notes: Boss encounters already feed run history and meta progression.

telemetry.notes: Combat logs and encounter history expose the behavior for debugging.

tests.notes: Engine-level tests are still needed for boss and directive behavior.

audio.notes: Boss phases and directive triggers need stronger audio identity.

narrative.notes: Event writing exists, but recurring NPC reactions and boss framing are still thin.

docs.notes: Contributor docs should capture where new directive logic belongs.

features.title: Combat pressure systems
features.summary: Heat, scrap salvaging/reforging, adaptive encounters, and arena hazards.

tutorials.notes: The new Pressure Systems tutorial now teaches arena modifiers, Heat, scrap recovery, and Reforge in a scripted flow.

ui.notes: Heat, scrap, and arena modifiers are now surfaced in the run and combat HUD.

content_balance.notes: The systems are parameterized and wired into encounter/balance flow.

ai.notes: Encounter weighting reacts to player strategy, and autoplay now understands Heat pressure, forge targeting, and scrap-spending rest/shop choices.

progression.notes: These systems do not yet feed unlocks, archives, or long-term run progression.

telemetry.notes: Adaptation profiles and combat logs capture the new behavior for debugging.

tests.notes: The new combat-meta helper has direct Node test coverage.

audio.notes: Heat spikes, salvage, and arena hazards still need dedicated feedback.

narrative.notes: A fixer or handler should comment on trace heat, salvage loops, and adaptive enemies.

docs.notes: Contributor docs and the README now call out the tutorial and autoplay retrofit points for these systems.
